description: Free from deceit; truthful and sincere. 'Wero' names the quality of straightforward truthfulness—speaking and acting without deception or hidden motives.
sound_symbolism: The word speaks straight. 'We' opens with clear intention, nothing hidden. Then 'ro' rolls forward directly, honesty complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'wero phelui' (honest words). Related to 'shewo' (true) but emphasizes the speaker's character rather than the statement's accuracy.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Right speech includes honesty—truth-telling that serves understanding and avoids harm.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Honest communication builds trust—relationships founded on truthfulness can weather difficulty.
